answers: Common beneficial supplements include Vitamin D, omega-3 fish oil, magnesium, and B12 (especially for vegetarians). However, getting nutrients from whole foods is always preferred over supplements.

Not everyone needs supplements. If you eat a balanced diet, you may get most nutrients from food. Vitamin D is one exception since many people are deficient, especially those with limited sun exposure.

Before taking supplements, get a blood test to identify actual deficiencies. Popular science-backed ones include Vitamin D3, omega-3, magnesium, and probiotics. Always buy from reputable brands and consult a healthcare provider, as some supplements can interact with medications.
